rationale: The LLM's demonstrated capability ceiling (intermediate web exploitation via SSTI) is almost entirely orthogonal to the extortion/negotiation dynamics of this step, which is driven by victim-side organizational factors (backup quality, cyber insurance, regulatory exposure, board risk appetite) that the attacker cannot influence through technical skill. LLM assistance provides marginal uplift through improved ransom communication drafting, victim financial research, and pressure-tactic optimization, but cannot overcome the fundamental constraint that ~70% of enterprises now decline to pay for reasons unrelated to attacker communication quality. The estimated uplift from 30% baseline to ~35% median reflects this narrow but real contribution to attacker effectiveness.
